summary:
The most common cause of interdental papilla protrusion under a rubber dam is improperly positioned dental punches placed too closely together, resulting in loss of papillary support and subsequent displacement. This mechanical issue differs fundamentally from pathological causes involving inflammation or infection affecting the gums.  

(Note: "D" was likely an option label; no actual options were provided.)

Okay, I see what you're asking—just keep it strictly factual and concise without adding interpretation or external details.

Final summary:

Improperly spaced dental punches lead to inadequate supporting tissues, causing the interdigital papillae to be displaced and project through the rubber dam margin. This mechanical failure during restorative procedures is more commonly observed than pathologic gum disorders related to inflammation.
